target.name: Conjour
target.tagline: AI-powered B2B messaging platform with personas, templates, content, and market intelligence.

uxSummary: Conjour is unmistakably positioned for B2B sales/marketing teams, not indie founders, and the signup flow is the first signal: company name plus first/last name plus work email plus password plus confirmation, all required. The brand palette is pink/magenta on a light background with the logomark sitting next to a typeface wordmark in the top-left. Authenticated, the sidebar reads as a complete B2B messaging workspace: Analytics, Messaging, Personas, Templates, Content, Market Expertise. The user identity in the top-right shows 'Lastest Demo Admin' with an Admin badge, meaning fresh signups land with admin privileges on their own workspace by default (the right call for solo-team starts). The 'Get started with Conjour' wizard that opens on first dashboard load is unusually well-thought-through: five labeled steps (Set up your brand, Messaging guide, Personas, Funnel + content, Intelligence sources), each with a Take tour button rather than a forced inline interaction. The Templates surface comes pre-populated with four messaging templates (Brand Personality, Campaign Theme, Product Marketing, Value Messaging) and a Persona Template, each broken into named blocks with character limits like 'Brand Personality (200 chars)'. That seeded content lifts the empty-state problem entirely for new users.

highlights.label: Five-step setup wizard with Take Tour, not forced
highlights.note: Most B2B SaaS wizards block the dashboard until completed. Conjour's 'Get started with Conjour' lists five labeled steps, each with a Take tour option and a self-serve 'we'll mark them done automatically as you go' progress signal. Users can dismiss and explore, the wizard reopens via 'Setup Wizard' in the user menu when wanted. Strong middle ground.

highlights.label: Templates pre-seeded with real structure
highlights.note: /templates renders four template cards (Brand Personality, Campaign Theme, Product Marketing, Value Messaging) plus a Persona Template, each listing the named messaging blocks with character budgets like '(200 chars)'. New users see exactly what good content looks like before writing any. Most messaging-platform empty states are 'create your first template' and nothing else.

highlights.label: Admin badge in chrome
highlights.note: Top-right shows 'Lastest Demo' with a pink Admin badge. New users land with admin rights on their own workspace, no role/seat ceremony for the first user. The badge being visually distinct means role-based testing is one screenshot away.

highlights.label: Market Expertise sidebar item
highlights.note: /market is labeled Market Expertise in the sidebar and renders 'Market Intelligence Updates' as the heading. Most B2B messaging tools end at content libraries; the Market Intelligence surface points to a planned competitive/news intake feature that's worth following.

frictionPoints.label: Console errors: Payment form not found
frictionPoints.note: Every authenticated route fires a console error 'Payment form not found' multiple times. The Stripe/payment integration is loading on routes that don't need it, and the integration is itself broken (zero outgoing payment-related fetch). For prospective customers running browser devtools open, this reads as 'this product has a broken integration'. Easy fix: gate the Stripe init on a billing/upgrade page rather than the global app shell.

frictionPoints.label: Wizard close button uses generic accessible name
frictionPoints.note: The 'Get started with Conjour' modal close button has accessible name 'close' (lowercase, generic) rather than 'Close setup wizard' or similar. A screen reader user hears just 'close button' with no context.

frictionPoints.label: Multi-field register form is enterprise-flavored
frictionPoints.note: Six required fields plus terms agreement on the register page. The B2B audience expects this; for the rare indie-curious visitor, it's a wall. A second 'Quick signup' flow (email plus magic link) would broaden the top of the funnel without disturbing the B2B-team flow.

testingStruggles.label: Account-exists detection needed register-to-login fallback
testingStruggles.note: On rerun, the same email triggers a 'this email is already in use' state on /accounts/register without obvious form-level error. First version of the test got stuck for 25 of 29 steps trying to fill the register form. Fixed by detecting the still-on-register URL after submit and falling through to /accounts/login with the same credentials.

testingStruggles.label: Wizard re-opens on dashboard load
testingStruggles.note: The 'Get started with Conjour' modal opens on /analytics (dashboard) on every fresh page load until 'Don't show this on login' is ticked. Test now dismisses it via a close button click immediately after auth, then re-dismisses on each subsequent route in case it reappears.

testingStruggles.label: Six form fields with mixed selectors
testingStruggles.note: Company Name and the password fields use input[name=...] attributes; First Name, Last Name, and Work Email use accessible-name-via-asterisk-label selectors. Test uses both strategies depending on the field. Brittleness risk: if Conjour relabels any of these, the test silently skips them.

skippedRoutes.reason: Would trigger product tours that modify workspace state. Wizard was dismissed instead.

skippedRoutes.path: Template edit / Persona create / Content upload
skippedRoutes.reason: All seeded templates and the create flows persist on the workspace. Walked the parent routes only.
Would write a research source to the user's database. Skipped.
